_invariant: Real USDC exposure graph at the 2023-03-11 SVB depeg. Token node anchored on ON-CHAIN reads (address/decimals/totalSupply at block 16803810 via Alchemy archive, ts 2023-03-11T09:00:11Z, totalSupply 38.926B, decimals 6). Two collateral edges into an isolated lending venue exercise both oracle regimes: one MARKET-price (depeg_sensitive=true — a sighted feed tracks the ~$0.87 secondary price and liquidates) and one HARDCODED $1.00 (depeg_sensitive=false — the typical blue-chip config; oracle blind, on-chain liquidations muted). LT=0.86 is a representative stablecoin-collateral liquidation line. The depeg price is injected per-case via peg_probes, not stored on the edge.

meta.data_source: on-chain Alchemy archive (USDC 0xA0b8…6eB48 @ block 16803810, ts 2023-03-11T09:00:11Z, totalSupply 38925924909579550 raw / 38.926B, decimals 6) + lending exposure from SVB-depeg post-mortems (Circle/Reuters/CoinDesk/Curve 3pool skew)

metadata._note: Fiat-backed stablecoin. ~$3.3B of reserves were at Silicon Valley Bank (FDIC receivership 2023-03-10). USDC traded as low as ~$0.87 on 2023-03-11 before the US Treasury/Fed/FDIC backstop (2023-03-12) restored confidence; recovered to ~0.999 by 2023-03-13. A backing-confidence depeg, not a permanent insolvency.
USDC was a top-3 collateral/borrow asset across lending venues with billions in aggregate exposure at the time of the depeg.
